NHS Acute Discharge Delays Analysis (2025–2026)¶

Project Overview¶

This project analyses NHS England Acute Daily Discharge Situation Report data for the 2025–2026 financial year.

The analysis focuses on acute discharge delays, including patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside, patients discharged, patients remaining in hospital, additional bed days lost, discharge destinations and delay reasons.

This project forms Phase 3 of a wider NHS operational analytics portfolio:

  1. NHS A&E Patient Flow Analysis
  2. NHS Bed Availability & Occupancy Analysis
  3. NHS Acute Discharge Delays Analysis

Together, these projects explore different parts of the hospital patient-flow pathway: demand into A&E, inpatient bed capacity, and discharge flow out of hospital.

Project Objectives¶

The objectives of this project are to:

  1. Analyse national acute discharge delay trends across 2025–26.
  2. Identify changes in patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside.
  3. Analyse patients discharged and patients remaining in hospital after no longer meeting criteria to reside.
  4. Explore regional, ICB and provider-level variation in discharge delays.
  5. Analyse additional bed days lost linked to delayed discharge.
  6. Examine discharge destinations and delay reasons.
  7. Prepare clean Tableau-ready datasets for dashboard development.

Dataset Information¶

This project uses NHS England Acute Daily Discharge Situation Report monthly CSV files for the 2025–26 financial year.

The files cover:

  • April 2025 to March 2026
  • National, regional, ICB and provider-level reporting
  • Daily discharge activity
  • Weekly snapshot metrics
  • Discharge destination information
  • Delay reason information

The dataset is provided in long format, with each row representing a reporting period, geography level, organisation, metric and value.

Why This Project Matters¶

Delayed discharge can create pressure across the hospital system by reducing available inpatient capacity and affecting patient flow.

Patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside but remain in hospital may contribute to wider capacity pressure, especially when combined with high bed occupancy and urgent care demand.

This analysis provides insight into the discharge side of the patient-flow pathway, complementing earlier analysis of A&E performance and bed occupancy.

Importing Libraries¶

The following libraries are used for data loading, cleaning, analysis, validation and visualisation.

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 100)
pd.set_option("display.max_rows", 100)
pd.set_option("display.max_colwidth", 120)

Setting Project Paths¶

The project folder contains the monthly CSV files in a data folder and saves analysis outputs to the outputs folder.

In [2]:
# Set the main project folder.

project_path = r"C:\Users\raahu\OneDrive\Desktop\nhs-discharge-delays-sitrep-analysis"

if not os.path.exists(project_path):
    project_path = os.getcwd()

data_path = os.path.join(project_path, "data")

# Fallback for environments where the CSV files are stored in the same folder as the notebook
if not os.path.exists(data_path):
    data_path = project_path

output_path = os.path.join(project_path, "outputs")
charts_path = os.path.join(output_path, "charts")
tables_path = os.path.join(output_path, "tables")
validation_path = os.path.join(output_path, "validation")
tableau_path = os.path.join(output_path, "tableau")

for path in [output_path, charts_path, tables_path, validation_path, tableau_path]:
    os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True)

print("Project path:", project_path)
print("Data path:", data_path)
print("Output path:", output_path)

Project path: C:\Users\raahu\OneDrive\Desktop\nhs-discharge-delays-sitrep-analysis
Data path: C:\Users\raahu\OneDrive\Desktop\nhs-discharge-delays-sitrep-analysis\data
Output path: C:\Users\raahu\OneDrive\Desktop\nhs-discharge-delays-sitrep-analysis\outputs

Loading NHS England Monthly CSV Files¶

The 12 monthly Acute Daily Discharge Situation Report CSV files are loaded and combined into a single raw dataset.

In [3]:
csv_files = {
    "apr_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv",
    "may_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_may_2025.csv",
    "jun_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_jun_2025.csv",
    "jul_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_jul_2025.csv",
    "aug_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_aug_2025.csv",
    "sep_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_sep_2025.csv",
    "oct_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_oct_2025.csv",
    "nov_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_nov_2025.csv",
    "dec_2025": "acute_discharge_sitrep_dec_2025.csv",
    "jan_2026": "acute_discharge_sitrep_jan_2026.csv",
    "feb_2026": "acute_discharge_sitrep_feb_2026.csv",
    "mar_2026": "acute_discharge_sitrep_mar_2026.csv"
}

for month_key, file_name in csv_files.items():
    file_path = os.path.join(data_path, file_name)
    print(month_key, "exists:", os.path.exists(file_path))

apr_2025 exists: True
may_2025 exists: True
jun_2025 exists: True
jul_2025 exists: True
aug_2025 exists: True
sep_2025 exists: True
oct_2025 exists: True
nov_2025 exists: True
dec_2025 exists: True
jan_2026 exists: True
feb_2026 exists: True
mar_2026 exists: True
In [4]:
csv_dataframes = []

for month_key, file_name in csv_files.items():
    file_path = os.path.join(data_path, file_name)
    
    temp_df = pd.read_csv(file_path)
    temp_df["file_month"] = month_key
    temp_df["source_file"] = file_name
    
    csv_dataframes.append(temp_df)

discharge_raw = pd.concat(csv_dataframes, ignore_index=True)

print("Combined raw shape:", discharge_raw.shape)
display(discharge_raw.head())

Initial Dataset Validation¶

Initial validation checks the raw structure, row counts, column names, data types and reporting date coverage across all monthly files.

In [7]:
discharge_raw["period_date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    discharge_raw["Period"],
    dayfirst=True,
    errors="coerce"
)

print("Invalid or missing dates:", discharge_raw["period_date"].isna().sum())

display(discharge_raw[["Period", "period_date", "file_month"]].head())

date_check = (
    discharge_raw
    .groupby("file_month", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        min_period_date=("period_date", "min"),
        max_period_date=("period_date", "max"),
        unique_dates=("period_date", "nunique")
    )
)

Date Validation and Data Quality Flags¶

Date validation is important because the monthly CSV files include a small number of expected weekly-boundary dates and a small number of out-of-month rows requiring review.

Rows are flagged rather than immediately removed so that the cleaning decision remains transparent.

In [9]:
expected_months = {
    "apr_2025": ("2025-04-01", "2025-04-30"),
    "may_2025": ("2025-05-01", "2025-05-31"),
    "jun_2025": ("2025-06-01", "2025-06-30"),
    "jul_2025": ("2025-07-01", "2025-07-31"),
    "aug_2025": ("2025-08-01", "2025-08-31"),
    "sep_2025": ("2025-09-01", "2025-09-30"),
    "oct_2025": ("2025-10-01", "2025-10-31"),
    "nov_2025": ("2025-11-01", "2025-11-30"),
    "dec_2025": ("2025-12-01", "2025-12-31"),
    "jan_2026": ("2026-01-01", "2026-01-31"),
    "feb_2026": ("2026-02-01", "2026-02-28"),
    "mar_2026": ("2026-03-01", "2026-03-31")
}

start_dates = pd.to_datetime(discharge_raw["file_month"].map(lambda x: expected_months[x][0]))
end_dates = pd.to_datetime(discharge_raw["file_month"].map(lambda x: expected_months[x][1]))

discharge_raw["date_status"] = np.select(
    [
        discharge_raw["period_date"].isna(),
        (discharge_raw["period_date"] >= start_dates) & (discharge_raw["period_date"] <= end_dates),
        (discharge_raw["period_date"] < start_dates) & ((start_dates - discharge_raw["period_date"]).dt.days <= 7)
    ],
    [
        "Invalid or missing date",
        "Inside expected month",
        "Previous-month weekly boundary"
    ],
    default="Outside expected range"
)

date_status_summary = (
    discharge_raw
    .groupby(["file_month", "date_status"], as_index=False)
    .size()
    .rename(columns={"size": "row_count"})
)

display(date_status_summary)
file_month date_status row_count
0 apr_2025 Inside expected month 24696
1 apr_2025 Outside expected range 6216
2 apr_2025 Previous-month weekly boundary 504
3 aug_2025 Inside expected month 38136
4 dec_2025 Inside expected month 38136
5 feb_2026 Inside expected month 36624
6 jan_2026 Inside expected month 38136
7 jan_2026 Previous-month weekly boundary 504
8 jul_2025 Inside expected month 31416
9 jul_2025 Previous-month weekly boundary 504
10 jun_2025 Inside expected month 24696
11 jun_2025 Outside expected range 6384
12 mar_2026 Inside expected month 38136
13 may_2025 Inside expected month 31416
14 nov_2025 Inside expected month 37632
15 oct_2025 Inside expected month 38136
16 oct_2025 Previous-month weekly boundary 504
17 sep_2025 Inside expected month 37632
In [10]:
suspicious_dates = discharge_raw[
    discharge_raw["date_status"] == "Outside expected range"
].copy()

print("Suspicious rows:", suspicious_dates.shape[0])

display(
    suspicious_dates[
        [
            "file_month",
            "Period",
            "period_date",
            "Level",
            "Region",
            "ICB",
            "Org Code",
            "Org Name",
            "Metric Group",
            "Metric Type",
            "Metric",
            "Value",
            "source_file"
        ]
    ].head(100)
)

In [11]:
suspicious_summary = (
    suspicious_dates
    .groupby(["file_month", "Period", "Metric Group", "Metric Type"], as_index=False)
    .size()
    .rename(columns={"size": "row_count"})
    .sort_values(["file_month", "Period", "Metric Group"])
)

display(suspicious_summary)
file_month Period Metric Group Metric Type row_count
0 apr_2025 01/10/2025 Delay reason LOS 7+ Weekly snapshot metric 6216
1 jun_2025 01/05/2025 NCTR Daily metric 168
2 jun_2025 01/10/2025 Delay reason LOS 7+ Weekly snapshot metric 6216
In [12]:
suspicious_value_check = (
    suspicious_dates
    .groupby(["file_month", "Period", "Metric Group"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        row_count=("Value", "count"),
        missing_values=("Value", lambda x: x.isna().sum()),
        unique_metrics=("Metric", "nunique"),
        unique_orgs=("Org Code", "nunique")
    )
)

display(suspicious_value_check)
file_month Period Metric Group row_count missing_values unique_metrics unique_orgs
0 apr_2025 01/10/2025 Delay reason LOS 7+ 6216 0 37 168
1 jun_2025 01/05/2025 NCTR 168 0 1 168
2 jun_2025 01/10/2025 Delay reason LOS 7+ 6216 0 37 168
In [13]:
june_nctr_suspicious = suspicious_dates[
    (suspicious_dates["file_month"] == "jun_2025") &
    (suspicious_dates["Metric Group"] == "NCTR")
].copy()

display(
    june_nctr_suspicious[
        [
            "file_month",
            "Period",
            "period_date",
            "Level",
            "Region",
            "ICB",
            "Org Code",
            "Org Name",
            "Metric Group",
            "Metric Type",
            "Metric",
            "Value",
            "source_file"
        ]
    ].head(50)
)

print("Unique metrics:")
print(june_nctr_suspicious["Metric"].unique())

print("\nValue summary:")
display(june_nctr_suspicious["Value"].value_counts(dropna=False).head(20))
file_month Period period_date Level Region ICB Org Code Org Name Metric Group Metric Type Metric Value source_file

In [14]:
reporting_month_map = {
    "apr_2025": "2025-04-01",
    "may_2025": "2025-05-01",
    "jun_2025": "2025-06-01",
    "jul_2025": "2025-07-01",
    "aug_2025": "2025-08-01",
    "sep_2025": "2025-09-01",
    "oct_2025": "2025-10-01",
    "nov_2025": "2025-11-01",
    "dec_2025": "2025-12-01",
    "jan_2026": "2026-01-01",
    "feb_2026": "2026-02-01",
    "mar_2026": "2026-03-01"
}

reporting_quarter_map = {
    "apr_2025": "Q1",
    "may_2025": "Q1",
    "jun_2025": "Q1",
    "jul_2025": "Q2",
    "aug_2025": "Q2",
    "sep_2025": "Q2",
    "oct_2025": "Q3",
    "nov_2025": "Q3",
    "dec_2025": "Q3",
    "jan_2026": "Q4",
    "feb_2026": "Q4",
    "mar_2026": "Q4"
}

discharge_raw["reporting_month"] = pd.to_datetime(
    discharge_raw["file_month"].map(reporting_month_map)
)

discharge_raw["reporting_quarter"] = discharge_raw["file_month"].map(reporting_quarter_map)

discharge_raw["reporting_month_label"] = discharge_raw["reporting_month"].dt.strftime("%b %Y")

display(
    discharge_raw[
        ["file_month", "Period", "period_date", "reporting_month", "reporting_month_label", "reporting_quarter", "date_status"]
    ].head()
)
file_month Period period_date reporting_month reporting_month_label reporting_quarter date_status
0 apr_2025 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 Inside expected month
1 apr_2025 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 Inside expected month
2 apr_2025 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 Inside expected month
3 apr_2025 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 Inside expected month
4 apr_2025 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 Inside expected month
In [15]:
discharge_raw["data_quality_flag"] = "Review"

discharge_raw.loc[
    discharge_raw["date_status"] == "Inside expected month",
    "data_quality_flag"
] = "Valid reporting-month row"

discharge_raw.loc[
    discharge_raw["date_status"] == "Previous-month weekly boundary",
    "data_quality_flag"
] = "Valid weekly boundary row"

# April and June CSV files contain LOS 7+ delay reason rows labelled 01/10/2025.
# These rows matched the official monthly Excel publications during validation, so they are retained and flagged.
corrected_delay_reason_label = (
    discharge_raw["file_month"].isin(["apr_2025", "jun_2025"]) &
    (discharge_raw["Period"] == "01/10/2025") &
    (discharge_raw["Metric Group"] == "Delay reason LOS 7+")
)

discharge_raw.loc[
    corrected_delay_reason_label,
    "data_quality_flag"
] = "Corrected CSV date-label issue"

# June also contains out-of-month NCTR rows dated 01/05/2025 with non-numeric values.
# These are excluded from the clean analytical dataset.
june_out_of_month_nctr = (
    (discharge_raw["file_month"] == "jun_2025") &
    (discharge_raw["Period"] == "01/05/2025") &
    (discharge_raw["Metric Group"] == "NCTR")
)

discharge_raw.loc[
    june_out_of_month_nctr,
    "data_quality_flag"
] = "Exclude - suspicious duplicate/out-of-month NCTR row"

quality_summary = (
    discharge_raw
    .groupby(["file_month", "data_quality_flag"], as_index=False)
    .size()
    .rename(columns={"size": "row_count"})
)

display(quality_summary)
file_month data_quality_flag row_count
0 apr_2025 Corrected CSV date-label issue 6216
1 apr_2025 Valid reporting-month row 24696
2 apr_2025 Valid weekly boundary row 504
3 aug_2025 Valid reporting-month row 38136
4 dec_2025 Valid reporting-month row 38136
5 feb_2026 Valid reporting-month row 36624
6 jan_2026 Valid reporting-month row 38136
7 jan_2026 Valid weekly boundary row 504
8 jul_2025 Valid reporting-month row 31416
9 jul_2025 Valid weekly boundary row 504
10 jun_2025 Corrected CSV date-label issue 6216
11 jun_2025 Exclude - suspicious duplicate/out-of-month NCTR row 168
12 jun_2025 Valid reporting-month row 24696
13 mar_2026 Valid reporting-month row 38136
14 may_2025 Valid reporting-month row 31416
15 nov_2025 Valid reporting-month row 37632
16 oct_2025 Valid reporting-month row 38136
17 oct_2025 Valid weekly boundary row 504
18 sep_2025 Valid reporting-month row 37632

Cleaning the Dataset¶

The cleaning process removes rows that were identified as suspicious duplicate/out-of-month NCTR records, standardises column names and creates a numeric value field for analysis.

In [16]:
discharge_clean = discharge_raw[
    discharge_raw["data_quality_flag"] != "Exclude - suspicious duplicate/out-of-month NCTR row"
].copy()

print("Raw rows:", discharge_raw.shape[0])
print("Clean rows:", discharge_clean.shape[0])
print("Rows removed:", discharge_raw.shape[0] - discharge_clean.shape[0])

display(
    discharge_clean["data_quality_flag"]
    .value_counts()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"index": "data_quality_flag", "data_quality_flag": "row_count"})
)

Raw rows: 429408
Clean rows: 429240
Rows removed: 168
row_count count
0 Valid reporting-month row 414792
1 Corrected CSV date-label issue 12432
2 Valid weekly boundary row 2016
In [17]:
discharge_clean = discharge_clean.rename(
    columns={
        "Period": "period",
        "Level": "level",
        "Region": "region",
        "ICB": "icb",
        "Org Code": "org_code",
        "Org Name": "org_name",
        "Metric": "metric",
        "Metric Type": "metric_type",
        "Metric Group": "metric_group",
        "Value": "value"
    }
)

print(discharge_clean.columns.tolist())
display(discharge_clean.head())

['period', 'level', 'region', 'icb', 'org_code', 'org_name', 'metric', 'metric_type', 'metric_group', 'value', 'file_month', 'source_file', 'period_date', 'date_status', 'reporting_month', 'reporting_quarter', 'reporting_month_label', 'data_quality_flag']

2 01/04/2025 Region LONDON [z] Y56 LONDON Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside Daily metric NCTR 3285 apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv 2025-04-01 Inside expected month 2025-04-01 Q1 Apr 2025 Valid reporting-month row

In [18]:
text_cols = [
    "period",
    "level",
    "region",
    "icb",
    "org_code",
    "org_name",
    "metric",
    "metric_type",
    "metric_group",
    "file_month",
    "source_file",
    "date_status",
    "data_quality_flag",
    "reporting_quarter",
    "reporting_month_label"
]

for col in text_cols:
    discharge_clean[col] = discharge_clean[col].astype(str).str.strip()

display(discharge_clean[text_cols].head())
period level region icb org_code org_name metric metric_type metric_group file_month source_file date_status data_quality_flag reporting_quarter reporting_month_label
0 01/04/2025 National [z] [z] ENG ENGLAND (Type 1 Trusts) Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside Daily metric NCTR apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv Inside expected month Valid reporting-month row Q1 Apr 2025
1 01/04/2025 Region EAST OF ENGLAND [z] Y61 EAST OF ENGLAND Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside Daily metric NCTR apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv Inside expected month Valid reporting-month row Q1 Apr 2025
2 01/04/2025 Region LONDON [z] Y56 LONDON Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside Daily metric NCTR apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv Inside expected month Valid reporting-month row Q1 Apr 2025
3 01/04/2025 Region MIDLANDS [z] Y60 MIDLANDS Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside Daily metric NCTR apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv Inside expected month Valid reporting-month row Q1 Apr 2025
4 01/04/2025 Region NORTH EAST AND YORKSHIRE [z] Y63 NORTH EAST AND YORKSHIRE Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside Daily metric NCTR apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv Inside expected month Valid reporting-month row Q1 Apr 2025
In [19]:
discharge_clean["value_clean"] = (
    discharge_clean["value"]
    .replace("-", np.nan)
    .replace("[z]", np.nan)
)

discharge_clean["value_clean"] = pd.to_numeric(
    discharge_clean["value_clean"],
    errors="coerce"
)

print("Original value column type:", discharge_clean["value"].dtype)
print("Clean value column type:", discharge_clean["value_clean"].dtype)

print("\nMissing numeric values after cleaning:")
print(discharge_clean["value_clean"].isna().sum())

display(
    discharge_clean[["value", "value_clean", "metric_group", "metric"]].head(20)
)

Original value column type: str
Clean value column type: float64

Missing numeric values after cleaning:
1422
value value_clean metric_group metric
0 24427 24427.0 NCTR Number of patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside

In [20]:
missing_by_metric_group = (
    discharge_clean
    .groupby("metric_group", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_rows=("value_clean", "size"),
        missing_values=("value_clean", lambda x: x.isna().sum()),
        non_missing_values=("value_clean", lambda x: x.notna().sum())
    )
)

missing_by_metric_group["missing_pct"] = (
    missing_by_metric_group["missing_values"] /
    missing_by_metric_group["total_rows"] * 100
).round(2)

display(missing_by_metric_group.sort_values("missing_pct", ascending=False))
metric_group total_rows missing_values non_missing_values missing_pct
7 NCTR 61320 420 60900 0.68
6 Discharges 61320 420 60900 0.68
8 NCTR not discharged 61320 420 60900 0.68
0 Additional bed days lost 26208 162 26046 0.62
1 Delay reason 24864 0 24864 0.00
4 Delay reason LOS 7+ Costs 53760 0 53760 0.00
3 Delay reason LOS 7+ 74592 0 74592 0.00
2 Delay reason LOS 14+ 49728 0 49728 0.00
5 Discharge destination 16128 0 16128 0.00
In [21]:
metric_group_summary = (
    discharge_clean
    .groupby(["metric_group", "metric_type"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        row_count=("value_clean", "size"),
        unique_metrics=("metric", "nunique"),
        non_missing_values=("value_clean", lambda x: x.notna().sum())
    )
    .sort_values(["metric_group", "metric_type"])
)

display(metric_group_summary)
metric_group metric_type row_count unique_metrics non_missing_values
0 Additional bed days lost Weekly snapshot metric 26208 3 26046
1 Delay reason Weekly snapshot metric 24864 37 24864
2 Delay reason LOS 14+ Weekly snapshot metric 49728 37 49728
3 Delay reason LOS 7+ Weekly snapshot metric 74592 37 74592
4 Delay reason LOS 7+ Costs Weekly snapshot metric 53760 40 53760
5 Discharge destination Daily metric 16128 8 16128
6 Discharges Daily metric 61320 1 60900
7 NCTR Daily metric 61320 1 60900
8 NCTR not discharged Daily metric 61320 1 60900

Creating Working Datasets¶

The cleaned dataset was separated into working datasets based on metric group. This allows daily discharge-flow metrics, weekly bed-day metrics, discharge destinations and delay reasons to be analysed separately.

The main working datasets are:

  • NCTR: patients who no longer meet the criteria to reside
  • Discharges: patients discharged
  • NCTR not discharged: patients who no longer meet criteria to reside but were not discharged
  • Additional bed days lost
  • Discharge destination
  • Delay reasons

Cost-related delay reason rows are retained separately but excluded from the main analysis to avoid mixing patient-count metrics with cost-related measures.

In [22]:
nctr = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"] == "NCTR"
].copy()

discharges = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"] == "Discharges"
].copy()

nctr_not_discharged = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"] == "NCTR not discharged"
].copy()

additional_bed_days = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"] == "Additional bed days lost"
].copy()

discharge_destination = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"] == "Discharge destination"
].copy()

delay_reason = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"].isin([
        "Delay reason",
        "Delay reason LOS 7+",
        "Delay reason LOS 14+"
    ])
].copy()

delay_reason_costs = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"] == "Delay reason LOS 7+ Costs"
].copy()

print("NCTR:", nctr.shape)
print("Discharges:", discharges.shape)
print("NCTR not discharged:", nctr_not_discharged.shape)
print("Additional bed days lost:", additional_bed_days.shape)
print("Discharge destination:", discharge_destination.shape)
print("Delay reason:", delay_reason.shape)
print("Delay reason costs:", delay_reason_costs.shape)

NCTR: (61320, 19)
Discharges: (61320, 19)
NCTR not discharged: (61320, 19)
Additional bed days lost: (26208, 19)
Discharge destination: (16128, 19)
Delay reason: (149184, 19)
Delay reason costs: (53760, 19)
In [23]:
working_datasets = {
    "NCTR": nctr,
    "Discharges": discharges,
    "NCTR not discharged": nctr_not_discharged,
    "Additional bed days lost": additional_bed_days,
    "Discharge destination": discharge_destination,
    "Delay reason": delay_reason,
    "Delay reason costs": delay_reason_costs
}

level_summary_list = []

for dataset_name, df in working_datasets.items():
    temp = (
        df.groupby("level", as_index=False)
        .size()
        .rename(columns={"size": "row_count"})
    )
    temp["dataset"] = dataset_name
    level_summary_list.append(temp)

level_summary = pd.concat(level_summary_list, ignore_index=True)

level_summary = level_summary[
    ["dataset", "level", "row_count"]
].sort_values(["dataset", "level"])

display(level_summary)
dataset level row_count

In [24]:
date_coverage_list = []

for dataset_name, df in working_datasets.items():
    temp = (
        df.groupby("file_month", as_index=False)
        .agg(
            min_period_date=("period_date", "min"),
            max_period_date=("period_date", "max"),
            row_count=("value_clean", "size"),
            non_missing_values=("value_clean", lambda x: x.notna().sum())
        )
    )
    temp["dataset"] = dataset_name
    date_coverage_list.append(temp)

date_coverage_summary = pd.concat(date_coverage_list, ignore_index=True)

display(
    date_coverage_summary.sort_values(["dataset", "file_month"])
)
file_month min_period_date max_period_date row_count non_missing_values dataset

Creating Daily NCTR Flow Metrics¶

The daily NCTR flow dataset combines three daily metrics into one wide analytical table:

  • patients who no longer met the criteria to reside
  • patients discharged
  • patients who no longer met the criteria to reside but were not discharged

The calculated discharge-rate measures in this notebook refer only to the NCTR population. They should be interpreted as the proportion of NCTR patients discharged or not discharged, not as an overall hospital discharge rate.

In [25]:
daily_flow_metrics = discharge_clean[
    discharge_clean["metric_group"].isin([
        "NCTR",
        "Discharges",
        "NCTR not discharged"
    ])
].copy()

daily_flow_wide = daily_flow_metrics.pivot_table(
    index=[
        "period_date",
        "period",
        "reporting_month",
        "reporting_month_label",
        "reporting_quarter",
        "file_month",
        "level",
        "region",
        "icb",
        "org_code",
        "org_name"
    ],
    columns="metric_group",
    values="value_clean",
    aggfunc="sum"
).reset_index()

daily_flow_wide.columns.name = None

daily_flow_wide = daily_flow_wide.rename(
    columns={
        "NCTR": "nctr_patients",
        "Discharges": "patients_discharged",
        "NCTR not discharged": "nctr_not_discharged"
    }
)

daily_flow_wide["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    daily_flow_wide["nctr_not_discharged"] /
    daily_flow_wide["nctr_patients"] * 100
)

daily_flow_wide["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    daily_flow_wide["patients_discharged"] /
    daily_flow_wide["nctr_patients"] * 100
)

print("Daily flow wide shape:", daily_flow_wide.shape)

display(daily_flow_wide.head())

Daily flow wide shape: (61320, 16)
period_date period reporting_month reporting_month_label reporting_quarter file_month level region icb org_code org_name patients_discharged nctr_patients nctr_not_discharged nctr_not_discharged_rate nctr_discharged_rate

3 2025-04-01 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 apr_2025 ICB EAST OF ENGLAND NHS MID AND SOUTH ESSEX INTEGRATED CARE BOARD QH8 NHS MID AND SOUTH ESSEX INTEGRATED CARE BOARD 279.0 365.0 86.0 23.561644 76.438356
4 2025-04-01 01/04/2025 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 apr_2025 ICB EAST OF ENGLAND NHS NORFOLK AND WAVENEY INTEGRATED CARE BOARD QMM NHS NORFOLK AND WAVENEY INTEGRATED CARE BOARD 212.0 359.0 147.0 40.947075 59.052925
In [26]:
daily_flow_quality = daily_flow_wide[
    [
        "nctr_patients",
        "patients_discharged",
        "nctr_not_discharged",
        "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
        "nctr_discharged_rate"
    ]
].isna().sum()

display(daily_flow_quality)

print("Rows where NCTR is missing:")
display(daily_flow_wide[daily_flow_wide["nctr_patients"].isna()].head())

print("Rows where NCTR is zero:")
display(daily_flow_wide[daily_flow_wide["nctr_patients"] == 0].head())

National Acute Discharge Trends¶

This section analyses national daily and monthly trends in patients who no longer met the criteria to reside, patients discharged and patients not discharged.

In [27]:
national_daily_flow = daily_flow_wide[
    daily_flow_wide["level"] == "National"
].copy()

national_daily_flow = national_daily_flow.sort_values("period_date")

display(national_daily_flow.head())
display(national_daily_flow.tail())

print("National daily rows:", national_daily_flow.shape[0])
period_date period reporting_month reporting_month_label reporting_quarter file_month level region icb org_code org_name patients_discharged nctr_patients nctr_not_discharged nctr_not_discharged_rate nctr_discharged_rate

60858 2026-03-29 29/03/2026 2026-03-01 Mar 2026 Q4 mar_2026 National [z] [z] ENG ENGLAND (Type 1 Trusts) 5590.0 18742.0 13152.0 70.173941 29.826059
61026 2026-03-30 30/03/2026 2026-03-01 Mar 2026 Q4 mar_2026 National [z] [z] ENG ENGLAND (Type 1 Trusts) 8773.0 21903.0 13130.0 59.946126 40.053874
61194 2026-03-31 31/03/2026 2026-03-01 Mar 2026 Q4 mar_2026 National [z] [z] ENG ENGLAND (Type 1 Trusts) 10475.0 23866.0 13391.0 56.109109 43.890891 
National daily rows: 365
In [28]:
national_monthly_summary = (
    national_daily_flow
    .groupby(["reporting_month", "reporting_month_label", "reporting_quarter"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("nctr_patients", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("patients_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("nctr_patients", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("patients_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "sum")
    )
)

national_monthly_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    national_monthly_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    national_monthly_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

national_monthly_summary["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    national_monthly_summary["total_discharged"] /
    national_monthly_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

national_monthly_summary = national_monthly_summary.sort_values("reporting_month")

display(national_monthly_summary)
reporting_month reporting_month_label reporting_quarter avg_daily_nctr avg_daily_discharged avg_daily_not_discharged total_nctr total_discharged total_not_discharged nctr_not_discharged_rate nctr_discharged_rate
0 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 22541.633333 9801.433333 12740.200000 676249.0 294043.0 382206.0 56.518531 43.481469
1 2025-05-01 May 2025 Q1 22602.935484 9930.741935 12672.193548 700691.0 307853.0 392838.0 56.064371 43.935629

In [29]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

plt.plot(
    national_monthly_summary["reporting_month_label"],
    national_monthly_summary["avg_daily_nctr"],
    marker="o",
    label="Avg daily NCTR patients"
)

plt.plot(
    national_monthly_summary["reporting_month_label"],
    national_monthly_summary["avg_daily_not_discharged"],
    marker="o",
    label="Avg daily NCTR not discharged"
)

plt.title("National Average Daily NCTR Patients and Not Discharged Patients")
plt.xlabel("Reporting month")
plt.ylabel("Average daily patients")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

plt.plot(
    national_monthly_summary["reporting_month_label"],
    national_monthly_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"],
    marker="o"
)

plt.title("National NCTR Not-Discharged Rate for Patients No Longer Meeting Criteria to Reside")
plt.xlabel("Reporting month")
plt.ylabel("NCTR not-discharged rate (%)")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
In [31]:
national_quarterly_summary = (
    national_monthly_summary
    .groupby("reporting_quarter", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("avg_daily_nctr", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("avg_daily_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("avg_daily_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("total_nctr", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("total_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("total_not_discharged", "sum")
    )
)

national_quarterly_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    national_quarterly_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    national_quarterly_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

national_quarterly_summary["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    national_quarterly_summary["total_discharged"] /
    national_quarterly_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

quarter_order = {"Q1": 1, "Q2": 2, "Q3": 3, "Q4": 4}
national_quarterly_summary["quarter_order"] = national_quarterly_summary["reporting_quarter"].map(quarter_order)

national_quarterly_summary = national_quarterly_summary.sort_values("quarter_order")

display(national_quarterly_summary)
reporting_quarter avg_daily_nctr avg_daily_discharged avg_daily_not_discharged total_nctr total_discharged total_not_discharged nctr_not_discharged_rate nctr_discharged_rate quarter_order
0 Q1 22382.967384 9888.413978 12494.553405 2037070.0 899888.0 1137182.0 55.824395 44.175605 1
1 Q2 22513.665233 9879.528315 12634.136918 2071117.0 908957.0 1162160.0 56.112716 43.887284 2
2 Q3 22801.174194 9756.321147 13044.853047 2097777.0 897785.0 1199992.0 57.203030 42.796970 3
3 Q4 23216.129416 9461.236943 13754.892473 2088922.0 851381.0 1237541.0 59.243045 40.756955 4

National Findings¶

The national daily discharge-flow data shows that a large proportion of patients who no longer met the criteria to reside remained in hospital during 2025–26.

Across the year, the national NCTR not-discharged rate generally remained above 54%. The rate increased towards the end of the financial year, reaching around 59% in Q4.

This suggests that discharge-flow pressure became more visible in the later months of 2025–26. However, this analysis does not prove a direct causal relationship between delayed discharge, bed occupancy or A&E performance.

Quarterly National Trend¶

The quarterly national summary shows a clear increase in discharge-flow pressure across 2025–26.

The NCTR not-discharged rate increased from 55.8% in Q1 to 59.2% in Q4. During the same period, the average daily number of patients who no longer met the criteria to reside increased, while the average daily number of NCTR patients discharged decreased.

This suggests that discharge delays became more pronounced toward the end of the financial year, with a larger proportion of patients remaining in hospital after no longer meeting the criteria to reside.

Regional Discharge Delay Analysis¶

Regional analysis explores whether discharge-flow pressure was evenly distributed across NHS England regions or concentrated in specific areas.

In [32]:
regional_daily_flow = daily_flow_wide[
    daily_flow_wide["level"] == "Region"
].copy()

print("Regional daily rows:", regional_daily_flow.shape[0])
display(regional_daily_flow.head())

In [33]:
regional_monthly_summary = (
    regional_daily_flow
    .groupby(
        ["region", "reporting_month", "reporting_month_label", "reporting_quarter"],
        as_index=False
    )
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("nctr_patients", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("patients_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("nctr_patients", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("patients_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "sum")
    )
)

regional_monthly_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    regional_monthly_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    regional_monthly_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

regional_monthly_summary["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    regional_monthly_summary["total_discharged"] /
    regional_monthly_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

regional_monthly_summary = regional_monthly_summary.sort_values(
    ["region", "reporting_month"]
)

display(regional_monthly_summary.head(20))

In [34]:
regional_year_summary = (
    regional_monthly_summary
    .groupby("region", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("avg_daily_nctr", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("avg_daily_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("avg_daily_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("total_nctr", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("total_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("total_not_discharged", "sum")
    )
)

regional_year_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    regional_year_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    regional_year_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

regional_year_summary["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    regional_year_summary["total_discharged"] /
    regional_year_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

regional_year_summary = regional_year_summary.sort_values(
    "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
    ascending=False
)

display(regional_year_summary)

    .groupby("region", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("avg_daily_nctr", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("avg_daily_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("avg_daily_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("total_nctr", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("total_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("total_not_discharged", "sum")
    )
)

regional_q4_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    regional_q4_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    regional_q4_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

    regional_q4_summary["total_discharged"] /
    regional_q4_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

regional_q4_summary = regional_q4_summary.sort_values(
    "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
    ascending=False
)

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

plt.bar(
    regional_year_summary["region"],
    regional_year_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"]
)

plt.title("Regional NCTR Not-Discharged Rate for NCTR Patients, 2025–26")
plt.xlabel("NHS region")
plt.ylabel("NCTR not-discharged rate (%)")
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
In [37]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

plt.bar(
    regional_q4_summary["region"],
    regional_q4_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"]
)

plt.title("Regional NCTR Not-Discharged Rate for NCTR Patients in Q4")
plt.xlabel("NHS region")
plt.ylabel("NCTR not-discharged rate (%)")
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
In [38]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

for region in regional_monthly_summary["region"].unique():
    temp = regional_monthly_summary[
        regional_monthly_summary["region"] == region
    ]
    
    plt.plot(
        temp["reporting_month_label"],
        temp["nctr_not_discharged_rate"],
        marker="o",
        label=region
    )

plt.title("Monthly NCTR Not-Discharged Rate by NHS Region")
plt.xlabel("Reporting month")
plt.ylabel("NCTR not-discharged rate (%)")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

Regional Findings¶

Regional analysis shows that discharge-flow pressure was not evenly distributed across NHS regions during 2025–26.

Across the full financial year, the South East had the highest annual NCTR not-discharged rate, followed by the North West and South West. The East of England had the lowest NCTR not-discharged rate and the highest NCTR discharged rate.

Q4 showed particularly high regional discharge-flow pressure. Several regions had NCTR not-discharged rates above 60%, including the South East, North East and Yorkshire, North West and London.

ICB-Level Discharge Delay Analysis¶

ICB-level analysis provides a more detailed view of variation within and across regions.

In [39]:
icb_daily_flow = daily_flow_wide[
    daily_flow_wide["level"] == "ICB"
].copy()

print("ICB daily rows:", icb_daily_flow.shape[0])
display(icb_daily_flow.head())

icb_year_summary = (
    icb_daily_flow
    .groupby(["region", "icb", "org_code", "org_name"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("nctr_patients", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("patients_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("nctr_patients", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("patients_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "sum")
    )
)

icb_year_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    icb_year_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    icb_year_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

icb_year_summary["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    icb_year_summary["total_discharged"] /
    icb_year_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

icb_year_summary = icb_year_summary.sort_values(
    "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
    ascending=False
)

display(icb_year_summary.head(15))

In [42]:
top15_icb_not_discharged = icb_year_summary.head(15).copy()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))

plt.barh(
    top15_icb_not_discharged["org_name"],
    top15_icb_not_discharged["nctr_not_discharged_rate"]
)

plt.title("Top 15 ICBs by NCTR Not-Discharged Rate, 2025–26")
plt.xlabel("NCTR not-discharged rate (%)")
plt.ylabel("ICB")
plt.gca().invert_yaxis()
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
In [43]:
icb_year_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "icb_discharge_delay_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

print("ICB summary saved.")

ICB summary saved.

ICB-Level Findings¶

ICB-level analysis shows substantial variation in the proportion of patients who no longer met the criteria to reside but were not discharged.

Across 2025–26, NHS Sussex Integrated Care Board had the highest NCTR not-discharged rate among ICBs, followed by NHS Northamptonshire Integrated Care Board, NHS Lincolnshire Integrated Care Board, NHS South West London Integrated Care Board and NHS Humber and North Yorkshire Integrated Care Board.

This suggests that discharge-flow pressure varied meaningfully within and across regions, highlighting the importance of analysing ICB-level patterns rather than relying only on national or regional averages.

Provider-Level Discharge Delay Analysis¶

Provider-level analysis explores variation between acute providers. To support fairer comparison, annual provider rankings are restricted to organisations with at least 300 reporting days and at least 10,000 NCTR patients during 2025–26.

In [44]:
provider_daily_flow = daily_flow_wide[
    daily_flow_wide["level"] == "PROVIDER"
].copy()

print("Provider daily rows:", provider_daily_flow.shape[0])
display(provider_daily_flow.head())

In [45]:
provider_year_summary = (
    provider_daily_flow
    .groupby(["region", "icb", "org_code", "org_name"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        avg_daily_nctr=("nctr_patients", "mean"),
        avg_daily_discharged=("patients_discharged", "mean"),
        avg_daily_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "mean"),
        total_nctr=("nctr_patients", "sum"),
        total_discharged=("patients_discharged", "sum"),
        total_not_discharged=("nctr_not_discharged", "sum"),
        reporting_days=("period_date", "nunique")
    )
)

provider_year_summary["nctr_not_discharged_rate"] = (
    provider_year_summary["total_not_discharged"] /
    provider_year_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

provider_year_summary["nctr_discharged_rate"] = (
    provider_year_summary["total_discharged"] /
    provider_year_summary["total_nctr"] * 100
)

provider_year_summary = provider_year_summary.sort_values(
    "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
    ascending=False
)

display(provider_year_summary.head(20))
region icb org_code org_name avg_daily_nctr avg_daily_discharged avg_daily_not_discharged total_nctr total_discharged total_not_discharged reporting_days nctr_not_discharged_rate nctr_discharged_rate

        (provider_year_summary["total_nctr"] >= 10000)

].copy()

display(
    excluded_providers.sort_values(
        ["reporting_days", "total_nctr"],
        ascending=[True, True]
    )
)

    top15_provider_not_discharged["org_name"],
    top15_provider_not_discharged["nctr_not_discharged_rate"]
)

In [50]:
provider_comparison.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "provider_discharge_delay_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

print("Provider discharge delay summary saved.")

Provider discharge delay summary saved.

Provider-Level Findings¶

Provider-level analysis shows substantial variation in discharge-flow pressure across NHS acute providers.

The provider-level threshold excluded one provider with only 30 reporting days. Among included providers, Northern Care Alliance NHS Foundation Trust had the highest NCTR not-discharged rate, followed by St George’s University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust and Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust.

These findings should be interpreted carefully. A high NCTR not-discharged rate does not necessarily indicate poorer clinical performance; it may reflect case mix, local discharge pathway capacity, social care availability, reporting practices and wider system pressures.

Additional Bed Days Lost Analysis¶

Additional bed days lost provide a capacity-impact view of discharge delays.

The dataset includes weekly snapshot metrics for patients with length of stay of 7 days or over, 14 days or over, and 21 days or over. These categories are overlapping rather than mutually exclusive, so they are not summed together for provider ranking.

The main bed-days analysis uses the LOS 7+ metric as the headline measure, while LOS 14+ and LOS 21+ are retained as indicators of longer-stay discharge delay pressure.

In [51]:
additional_bed_days_clean = additional_bed_days.copy()

print("Additional bed days rows:", additional_bed_days_clean.shape[0])

display(additional_bed_days_clean.head())

print("Unique metrics:")
print(additional_bed_days_clean["metric"].unique())

Additional bed days rows: 26208

15122 31/03/2025 Region LONDON [z] Y56 LONDON Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 7 days or over Weekly snapshot metric Additional bed days lost 20391 apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv 2025-03-31 Previous-month weekly boundary 2025-04-01 Q1 Apr 2025 Valid weekly boundary row 20391.0
15123 31/03/2025 Region MIDLANDS [z] Y60 MIDLANDS Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 7 days or over Weekly snapshot metric Additional bed days lost 12602 apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv 2025-03-31 Previous-month weekly boundary 2025-04-01 Q1 Apr 2025 Valid weekly boundary row 12602.0
15124 31/03/2025 Region NORTH EAST AND YORKSHIRE [z] Y63 NORTH EAST AND YORKSHIRE Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 7 days or over Weekly snapshot metric Additional bed days lost 17080 apr_2025 acute_discharge_sitrep_apr_2025.csv 2025-03-31 Previous-month weekly boundary 2025-04-01 Q1 Apr 2025 Valid weekly boundary row 17080.0 
Unique metrics:
<StringArray>
[ 'Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 7 days or over',
 'Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 14 days or over',
 'Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 21 days or over']
Length: 3, dtype: str
In [52]:
def bed_day_los_category(metric):
    if "7 days or over" in metric:
        return "LOS 7+"
    elif "14 days or over" in metric:
        return "LOS 14+"
    elif "21 days or over" in metric:
        return "LOS 21+"
    else:
        return "Other"

additional_bed_days_clean["los_category"] = additional_bed_days_clean["metric"].apply(bed_day_los_category)

display(
    additional_bed_days_clean[["metric", "los_category"]]
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values("los_category")
)
metric los_category
15960 Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 14 days or over LOS 14+
16800 Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 21 days or over LOS 21+
15120 Number of additional bed days, patients with length of stay of 7 days or over LOS 7+
In [53]:
national_bed_days = additional_bed_days_clean[
    additional_bed_days_clean["level"] == "National"
].copy()

national_bed_days_summary = (
    national_bed_days
    .groupby("los_category", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_additional_bed_days=("value_clean", "sum"),
        avg_weekly_additional_bed_days=("value_clean", "mean"),
        max_weekly_additional_bed_days=("value_clean", "max"),
        reporting_points=("period_date", "nunique")
    )
    .sort_values("total_additional_bed_days", ascending=False)
)

display(national_bed_days_summary)
los_category total_additional_bed_days avg_weekly_additional_bed_days max_weekly_additional_bed_days reporting_points
2 LOS 7+ 6744370.0 129699.423077 137816.0 52
0 LOS 14+ 6238506.0 119971.269231 127505.0 52
1 LOS 21+ 5534650.0 106435.576923 113621.0 52
In [54]:
national_bed_days_los7 = national_bed_days[
    national_bed_days["los_category"] == "LOS 7+"
].copy()

national_bed_days_los7_monthly = (
    national_bed_days_los7
    .groupby(["reporting_month", "reporting_month_label", "reporting_quarter"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        monthly_total_bed_days=("value_clean", "sum"),
        avg_weekly_bed_days=("value_clean", "mean"),
        max_weekly_bed_days=("value_clean", "max"),
        reporting_points=("period_date", "nunique")
    )
    .sort_values("reporting_month")
)

display(national_bed_days_los7_monthly)
reporting_month reporting_month_label reporting_quarter monthly_total_bed_days avg_weekly_bed_days max_weekly_bed_days reporting_points
0 2025-04-01 Apr 2025 Q1 627316.0 125463.20 130965.0 5

provider_bed_days_los7 = additional_bed_days_clean[
    (additional_bed_days_clean["level"] == "PROVIDER") &
    (additional_bed_days_clean["los_category"] == "LOS 7+")
].copy()

provider_bed_days_los7_summary = (
    provider_bed_days_los7
    .groupby(["region", "icb", "org_code", "org_name"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_los7_bed_days=("value_clean", "sum"),
        avg_weekly_los7_bed_days=("value_clean", "mean"),
        max_weekly_los7_bed_days=("value_clean", "max"),
        reporting_points=("period_date", "nunique")
    )
    .sort_values("total_los7_bed_days", ascending=False)
)

In [57]:
top15_provider_los7_bed_days = provider_bed_days_los7_summary.head(15).copy()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))

plt.barh(
    top15_provider_los7_bed_days["org_name"],
    top15_provider_los7_bed_days["total_los7_bed_days"]
)

plt.title("Top 15 Providers by Additional Bed Days for LOS 7+ Patients")
plt.xlabel("Total additional bed days")
plt.ylabel("Provider")
plt.gca().invert_yaxis()
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

Additional Bed Days Findings¶

At national level, the LOS 7+ metric recorded over 6.7 million additional bed days across 52 weekly reporting points in 2025–26. This suggests that delayed discharge represented a substantial capacity burden across the acute hospital system.

Provider-level additional bed days show the scale of capacity impact linked to delayed discharge. University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust recorded the highest LOS 7+ additional bed days total, followed by Leeds Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust and Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust.

This differs from the provider ranking by NCTR not-discharged rate, highlighting the difference between proportional discharge-flow pressure and total bed-day burden.

Delay Reason Analysis¶

Delay reason analysis provides insight into why patients with longer lengths of stay remained in hospital after no longer meeting the criteria to reside.

The main analysis focuses on LOS 7+ delay reasons because this provides the broadest view of delayed discharge pressure.

)

In [59]:
def standardise_delay_group(metric_group):
    if metric_group == "Delay reason":
        return "LOS 14+"
    elif metric_group == "Delay reason LOS 14+":
        return "LOS 14+"
    elif metric_group == "Delay reason LOS 7+":
        return "LOS 7+"
    else:
        return metric_group

delay_reason_clean["delay_los_group"] = delay_reason_clean["metric_group"].apply(
    standardise_delay_group
)

display(
    delay_reason_clean[["metric_group", "delay_los_group"]]
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values(["metric_group", "delay_los_group"])
)
metric_group delay_los_group
18984 Delay reason LOS 14+
144816 Delay reason LOS 14+ LOS 14+
25200 Delay reason LOS 7+ LOS 7+

national_delay_reasons_los7 = delay_reason_clean[
    (delay_reason_clean["level"] == "National") &
    (delay_reason_clean["delay_los_group"] == "LOS 7+")
].copy()

national_delay_reason_summary_los7 = (
    national_delay_reasons_los7
    .groupby("metric", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_value=("value_clean", "sum"),
        avg_monthly_value=("value_clean", "mean"),
        max_monthly_value=("value_clean", "max"),
        reporting_months=("reporting_month", "nunique")
    )
    .sort_values("total_value", ascending=False)
)

In [61]:
def shorten_delay_reason(reason):
    replacements = {
        "Care transfer hub process – Waiting for confirmation of immediate care needs and pathway":
            "CTH: awaiting care needs/pathway confirmation",
        "Capacity – Bed-based rehabilitation, reablement or recovery services not yet available (Pathway 2)":
            "Capacity: bed-based rehab/reablement unavailable",
        "Hospital process – Awaiting therapy review of need for supported discharge":
            "Hospital: awaiting therapy review",
        "Capacity – Residential/nursing home care not yet available (Pathway 3)":
            "Capacity: residential/nursing home unavailable",
        "Interface process – Residential/nursing home care arrangements still underway (Pathway 3)":
            "Interface: residential/nursing home arrangements",
        "Interface process – Bed-based rehabilitation, reablement or recovery service arrangements still underway (Pathway 2)":
            "Interface: bed-based rehab arrangements",
        "Capacity – Home-based rehabilitation, reablement or recovery services not yet available (Pathway 1)":
            "Capacity: home-based rehab/reablement unavailable",
        "Interface process – Other home-based social care service arrangements still underway (Pathway 1)":
            "Interface: home-based social care arrangements",
        "Hospital process – Awaiting referral to care transfer hub for supported discharge":
            "Hospital: awaiting referral to care transfer hub",
        "Interface process – Home based rehabilitation, reablement or recovery service arrangements still underway (Pathway 1)":
            "Interface: home-based rehab arrangements",
        "Care transfer hub process– Awaiting necessary referrals by care transfer hub":
            "CTH: awaiting necessary referrals",
        "Hospital process - Awaiting medical review of need for supported discharge":
            "Hospital: awaiting medical review",
        "Hospital process – Awaiting medicines to take home, discharge letter or other discharge documentation":
            "Hospital: medicines/discharge documentation",
        "Capacity – Other home-based social care service not yet available (Pathway 1)":
            "Capacity: home-based social care unavailable",
        "Hospital process – Awaiting formal decision to discharge (inc diagnostic test results)":
            "Hospital: awaiting formal discharge decision"
    }

    if reason in replacements:
        return replacements[reason]

    # Flexible fallbacks for closely related labels.
    if reason.startswith("Capacity") and "Home-based rehabilitation" in reason:
        return "Capacity: home-based rehab/reablement unavailable"
    if reason.startswith("Hospital process") and "medicines" in reason:
        return "Hospital: medicines/discharge documentation"

    return reason

delay_reason_clean["delay_reason_short"] = delay_reason_clean["metric"].apply(shorten_delay_reason)

national_delay_reason_summary_los7["delay_reason_short"] = (
    national_delay_reason_summary_los7["metric"].apply(shorten_delay_reason)
)

display(
    national_delay_reason_summary_los7[
        ["metric", "delay_reason_short", "total_value"]
    ].head(15)
)
metric delay_reason_short total_value

In [63]:
top5_delay_reason_names = national_delay_reason_summary_los7.head(5)["metric"].tolist()

national_delay_reason_monthly_los7 = (
    national_delay_reasons_los7[
        national_delay_reasons_los7["metric"].isin(top5_delay_reason_names)
    ]
    .groupby(["reporting_month", "reporting_month_label", "metric"], as_index=False)
    .agg(monthly_value=("value_clean", "sum"))
    .sort_values(["metric", "reporting_month"])
)

national_delay_reason_monthly_los7["delay_reason_short"] = (
    national_delay_reason_monthly_los7["metric"].apply(shorten_delay_reason)
)

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

for reason in national_delay_reason_monthly_los7["delay_reason_short"].unique():
    temp = national_delay_reason_monthly_los7[
        national_delay_reason_monthly_los7["delay_reason_short"] == reason
    ]

    plt.plot(
        temp["reporting_month_label"],
        temp["monthly_value"],
        marker="o",
        label=reason
    )

plt.title("Monthly Trend for Top 5 National LOS 7+ Delay Reasons")
plt.xlabel("Reporting month")
plt.ylabel("Monthly value")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
In [64]:
def delay_reason_category(reason):
    if reason.startswith("Capacity"):
        return "Capacity"
    elif reason.startswith("Interface process"):
        return "Interface process"
    elif reason.startswith("Hospital process"):
        return "Hospital process"
    elif reason.startswith("Care transfer hub process"):
        return "Care transfer hub process"
    elif reason.startswith("Care transfer hub process–"):
        return "Care transfer hub process"
    elif reason.startswith("Wellbeing concerns"):
        return "Wellbeing concerns"
    else:
        return "Other"

delay_reason_clean["delay_reason_category"] = delay_reason_clean["metric"].apply(delay_reason_category)

national_delay_reasons_los7 = delay_reason_clean[
    (delay_reason_clean["level"] == "National") &
    (delay_reason_clean["delay_los_group"] == "LOS 7+")
].copy()

national_delay_category_summary_los7 = (
    national_delay_reasons_los7
    .groupby("delay_reason_category", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_value=("value_clean", "sum"),
        avg_monthly_value=("value_clean", "mean"),
        unique_reasons=("metric", "nunique")
    )
    .sort_values("total_value", ascending=False)
)

display(national_delay_category_summary_los7)
delay_reason_category total_value avg_monthly_value unique_reasons
0 Capacity 44031.516667 333.572096 11
3 Interface process 31044.200000 517.403333 5
2 Hospital process 30219.016667 359.750198 7
1 Care transfer hub process 18741.200000 520.588889 3
4 Other 9477.983333 112.833135 7
5 Wellbeing concerns 7619.916667 158.748264 4

    national_delay_reasons_los14
    .groupby("delay_reason_category", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_value=("value_clean", "sum"),

)

display(national_delay_category_summary_los14)
delay_reason_category total_value avg_monthly_value unique_reasons
0 Capacity 33957.90 257.256818 11
3 Interface process 23973.30 399.555000 5
2 Hospital process 19125.35 227.682738 7
1 Care transfer hub process 13129.60 364.711111 3
4 Other 7481.05 89.060119 7
5 Wellbeing concerns 5819.60 121.241667 4

Delay Reason Findings¶

The top national LOS 7+ delay reasons included care transfer hub processes, limited availability of bed-based rehabilitation or reablement services, therapy review delays, and residential or nursing home care capacity.

Delay reason categories show that capacity-related reasons accounted for the largest share of LOS 7+ delayed discharge reasons nationally. The largest category was Capacity, followed by Interface process and Hospital process.

This suggests that acute discharge delays were shaped by a combination of onward-care capacity constraints, coordination across the hospital-community interface and internal hospital processes. It also shows that discharge delays are not only an acute hospital issue; they reflect wider system pressures across rehabilitation, reablement, residential and nursing home care, community services and care transfer processes.

LOS 14+ Delay Reason Check¶

A supporting check was completed for LOS 14+ delay reasons to understand whether longer-stay patients showed a different pattern.

The LOS 14+ results showed a similar structure to the LOS 7+ analysis. Capacity-related reasons remained the largest category, followed by interface processes, hospital processes and care transfer hub processes.

This suggests that longer-stay discharge delays were also strongly linked to onward-care capacity, rehabilitation and reablement availability, residential or nursing home capacity, and coordination across the hospital-community interface.

Discharge Destination Analysis¶

Discharge destination analysis shows where patients were discharged after no longer meeting the criteria to reside.

In [68]:

    discharge_destination_clean["metric"]
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

In [69]:
national_discharge_destination = discharge_destination_clean[
    discharge_destination_clean["level"] == "National"
].copy()

national_discharge_destination_summary = (
    national_discharge_destination
    .groupby("metric", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        total_value=("value_clean", "sum"),
        avg_monthly_value=("value_clean", "mean"),
        max_monthly_value=("value_clean", "max"),
        reporting_months=("reporting_month", "nunique")
    )
    .sort_values("total_value", ascending=False)
)

In [70]:
def shorten_discharge_destination(destination):
    if destination.startswith("Pathway 0: Discharge to a domestic home"):
        return "P0: home/temp accommodation, no new support"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 0: Discharge back to an original care home"):
        return "P0: return to original care home"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 1: Discharge to a domestic home") and "rehabilita" in destination:
        return "P1: home with rehab/reablement"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 1: Discharge to a domestic home"):
        return "P1: home with other new support"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 1: Discharge back to original care home"):
        return "P1: original care home with support"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 2"):
        return "P2: short-term bed/hospice"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 3: Discharge to a care home as a new admission"):
        return "P3: new care home admission"
    elif destination.startswith("Pathway 3: Discharge to a care home/hospice"):
        return "P3: care home/hospice EOL admission"
    else:
        return destination

discharge_destination_clean["destination_short"] = (
    discharge_destination_clean["metric"].apply(shorten_discharge_destination)
)

national_discharge_destination_summary["destination_short"] = (
    national_discharge_destination_summary["metric"].apply(shorten_discharge_destination)
)

display(
    national_discharge_destination_summary[
        ["metric", "destination_short", "total_value"]
    ]
)
metric destination_short total_value
1 Pathway 0: Discharge to a domestic home, hotel, or other temporary accommodation without the need for new/increased ... P0: home/temp accommodation, no new support 2963125.0
4 Pathway 1: Discharge to a domestic home, hotel, or other temporary accommodation, or hospice at home with rehabilita... P1: home with rehab/reablement 258059.0
5 Pathway 2: Short-term bed/hospice for rehabilitation, reablement and recovery / end of life care P2: short-term bed/hospice 114157.0
3 Pathway 1: Discharge to a domestic home, hotel, or other temporary accommodation, or hospice at home with other new/... P1: home with other new support 73622.0
0 Pathway 0: Discharge back to an original care home placement when the care home has confirmed they can continue to m... P0: return to original care home 67504.0
6 Pathway 3: Discharge to a care home as a new admission P3: new care home admission 44435.0
2 Pathway 1: Discharge back to original care home placement with rehabilitation, reablement and recovery or with an in... P1: original care home with support 20046.0
7 Pathway 3: Discharge to a care home/hospice as a new admission for end-of-life care P3: care home/hospice EOL admission 17063.0

    national_discharge_destination_summary["total_value"] /
    national_discharge_destination_summary["total_value"].sum() * 100
)

    national_discharge_destination_summary[
        ["destination_short", "total_value", "destination_share_pct"]

)

In [72]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

plt.bar(
    national_discharge_destination_summary["destination_short"],
    national_discharge_destination_summary["total_value"]
)

plt.title("National Discharge Destinations for NCTR Patients, 2025–26")
plt.xlabel("Discharge destination")
plt.ylabel("Total discharges")
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
In [73]:
top5_destination_names = (
    national_discharge_destination_summary
    .head(5)["metric"]
    .tolist()
)

national_destination_monthly = (
    national_discharge_destination[
        national_discharge_destination["metric"].isin(top5_destination_names)
    ]
    .groupby(["reporting_month", "reporting_month_label", "metric"], as_index=False)
    .agg(monthly_value=("value_clean", "sum"))
    .sort_values(["metric", "reporting_month"])
)

national_destination_monthly["destination_short"] = (
    national_destination_monthly["metric"].apply(shorten_discharge_destination)
)

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

for destination in national_destination_monthly["destination_short"].unique():
    temp = national_destination_monthly[
        national_destination_monthly["destination_short"] == destination
    ]

    plt.plot(
        temp["reporting_month_label"],
        temp["monthly_value"],
        marker="o",
        label=destination
    )

plt.title("Monthly Trend for Top 5 National Discharge Destinations")
plt.xlabel("Reporting month")
plt.ylabel("Monthly discharges")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

Discharge Destination Findings¶

Across 2025–26, the largest discharge destination category was Pathway 0: discharge to a domestic home, hotel or other temporary accommodation without the need for new or increased support. This accounted for 83.3% of recorded discharge destinations nationally.

More complex discharge pathways, including Pathway 1, Pathway 2 and Pathway 3, accounted for a much smaller share of recorded discharge destinations.

This suggests that most recorded discharges occurred through the least complex discharge route, while a smaller proportion required rehabilitation, reablement, short-term bed-based care, care home admission or end-of-life care pathways.

Exporting Clean Analytical Outputs¶

The main cleaned analytical summaries are exported to the outputs/tables folder for documentation, GitHub and future reuse.

In [74]:
# National outputs
national_monthly_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_monthly_discharge_flow_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

national_quarterly_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_quarterly_discharge_flow_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

# Regional and ICB outputs
regional_year_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "regional_discharge_delay_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

regional_q4_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "regional_q4_discharge_delay_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

icb_year_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "icb_discharge_delay_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

# Provider outputs
provider_comparison.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "provider_discharge_delay_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

provider_bed_days_los7_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "provider_los7_additional_bed_days_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

# Additional bed days outputs
national_bed_days_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_additional_bed_days_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

national_bed_days_los7_monthly.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_los7_additional_bed_days_monthly_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

# Delay reason outputs
national_delay_reason_summary_los7.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_los7_delay_reason_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

national_delay_category_summary_los7.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_los7_delay_reason_category_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

# Discharge destination outputs
national_discharge_destination_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tables_path, "national_discharge_destination_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

print("Main analysis outputs saved to:", tables_path)

Tableau Dashboard Preparation¶

The Tableau-ready outputs below are simplified CSV files designed for dashboard development. These outputs support national KPIs, geographic comparisons, provider rankings, bed-day burden, delay reasons and discharge destinations.

In [76]:
tableau_national_kpis = national_monthly_summary.copy()

    [
        "reporting_month",
        "reporting_month_label",
        "reporting_quarter",
        "avg_daily_nctr",
        "avg_daily_discharged",
        "avg_daily_not_discharged",
        "total_nctr",
        "total_discharged",
        "total_not_discharged",
        "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
        "nctr_discharged_rate"
    ]
].copy()

tableau_national_kpis.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tableau_path, "tableau_national_discharge_kpis_2025_26.csv"),

# Region level
tableau_region = regional_year_summary.copy()
tableau_region["geography_level"] = "Region"
tableau_region["geography_name"] = tableau_region["region"]
tableau_region["parent_region"] = tableau_region["region"]
tableau_region["parent_icb"] = np.nan

tableau_region = tableau_region[
    [
        "geography_level",
        "geography_name",
        "parent_region",
        "parent_icb",
        "avg_daily_nctr",
        "avg_daily_discharged",
        "avg_daily_not_discharged",
        "total_nctr",
        "total_discharged",
        "total_not_discharged",
        "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
        "nctr_discharged_rate"
    ]
]

# ICB level
tableau_icb = icb_year_summary.copy()
tableau_icb["geography_level"] = "ICB"
tableau_icb["geography_name"] = tableau_icb["org_name"]
tableau_icb["parent_region"] = tableau_icb["region"]
tableau_icb["parent_icb"] = tableau_icb["icb"]

tableau_icb = tableau_icb[
    [
        "geography_level",
        "geography_name",
        "parent_region",
        "parent_icb",
        "avg_daily_nctr",
        "avg_daily_discharged",
        "avg_daily_not_discharged",
        "total_nctr",
        "total_discharged",
        "total_not_discharged",
        "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
        "nctr_discharged_rate"
    ]
]

# Provider level
tableau_provider = provider_comparison.copy()
tableau_provider["geography_level"] = "Provider"
tableau_provider["geography_name"] = tableau_provider["org_name"]
tableau_provider["parent_region"] = tableau_provider["region"]
tableau_provider["parent_icb"] = tableau_provider["icb"]

tableau_provider = tableau_provider[
    [
        "geography_level",
        "geography_name",
        "parent_region",
        "parent_icb",
        "avg_daily_nctr",
        "avg_daily_discharged",
        "avg_daily_not_discharged",
        "total_nctr",
        "total_discharged",
        "total_not_discharged",
        "nctr_not_discharged_rate",
        "nctr_discharged_rate"
    ]
]

tableau_geography_summary = pd.concat(
    [tableau_region, tableau_icb, tableau_provider],
    ignore_index=True
)

tableau_geography_summary.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tableau_path, "tableau_geography_discharge_summary_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

tableau_delay_categories = national_delay_category_summary_los7.copy()

tableau_delay_categories.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tableau_path, "tableau_national_los7_delay_reason_categories_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

display(tableau_delay_reasons.head())
display(tableau_delay_categories)

tableau_discharge_destinations = national_discharge_destination_summary.copy()

tableau_discharge_destinations = tableau_discharge_destinations[
    [
        "metric",
        "destination_short",
        "total_value",
        "avg_monthly_value",
        "max_monthly_value",
        "reporting_months",
        "destination_share_pct"
    ]
].copy()

tableau_discharge_destinations.to_csv(
    os.path.join(tableau_path, "tableau_national_discharge_destinations_2025_26.csv"),
    index=False
)

print("Tableau-ready files:")

Tableau-ready files:
tableau_geography_discharge_summary_2025_26.csv
tableau_national_discharge_destinations_2025_26.csv
tableau_national_discharge_kpis_2025_26.csv
tableau_national_los7_bed_days_monthly_2025_26.csv
tableau_national_los7_delay_reason_categories_2025_26.csv
tableau_national_los7_delay_reasons_2025_26.csv
tableau_provider_los7_bed_days_2025_26.csv

Key Findings¶

This analysis identified several key findings from the NHS Acute Discharge SitRep data for 2025–26.

Nationally, a large proportion of patients who no longer met the criteria to reside remained in hospital. The national NCTR not-discharged rate increased from 55.8% in Q1 to 59.2% in Q4, suggesting that discharge-flow pressure became more pronounced toward the end of the financial year.

Regional analysis showed that discharge-flow pressure was not evenly distributed across NHS England regions. The South East had the highest annual NCTR not-discharged rate, followed by the North West and South West. In Q4, several regions had NCTR not-discharged rates above 60%, including the South East, North East and Yorkshire, North West and London.

ICB-level analysis showed further variation within regions. NHS Sussex Integrated Care Board had the highest annual NCTR not-discharged rate among ICBs, followed by NHS Northamptonshire Integrated Care Board and NHS Lincolnshire Integrated Care Board.

Provider-level analysis also showed substantial variation. Among providers with at least 300 reporting days and at least 10,000 NCTR patients, Northern Care Alliance NHS Foundation Trust had the highest NCTR not-discharged rate. However, this should be interpreted carefully, as provider-level variation may reflect local pathway capacity, case mix, reporting practices, social care availability and wider system pressures.

Additional bed days analysis showed that delayed discharge represented a substantial capacity burden. Nationally, the LOS 7+ additional bed days metric recorded more than 6.7 million additional bed days across 52 weekly reporting points in 2025–26.

Delay reason analysis showed that the largest category of LOS 7+ delay reasons was Capacity, followed by Interface process and Hospital process. This suggests that acute discharge delays were shaped by both internal hospital processes and wider system constraints, including rehabilitation, reablement, residential and nursing home care, community services and care transfer processes.

Discharge destination analysis showed that most recorded discharges were through Pathway 0, where patients were discharged to a domestic home, hotel or temporary accommodation without the need for new or increased support. This accounted for 83.3% of recorded discharge destinations nationally.

Limitations¶

This analysis is based on publicly available NHS England Acute Daily Discharge Situation Report data. The findings should be interpreted in the context of the dataset structure and reporting definitions.

The analysis is descriptive and does not prove causation. For example, higher NCTR not-discharged rates or additional bed days may be associated with wider system pressures, but this analysis cannot directly determine the causes of those pressures.

The calculated discharged and not-discharged rates refer only to patients who no longer met the criteria to reside. They should not be interpreted as overall hospital discharge rates.

Some metrics are reported as daily measures, while others are weekly snapshot measures. These were analysed separately to avoid mixing different reporting frequencies.

Additional bed days metrics for LOS 7+, LOS 14+ and LOS 21+ are overlapping categories. Therefore, they were not summed together for the main provider ranking. The LOS 7+ metric was used as the headline measure for additional bed days analysis.

Provider-level comparisons should be interpreted carefully. Differences may reflect local case mix, discharge pathway availability, social care capacity, community service provision, reporting practices and organisational context.

The dataset does not include patient-level information, clinical complexity, local operational context or detailed social care capacity data. Therefore, the analysis can identify patterns and variation but cannot fully explain the underlying reasons for all observed differences.

Conclusion¶

This project analysed NHS acute discharge delays across the 2025–26 financial year, focusing on patients who no longer met the criteria to reside, NCTR discharged and not-discharged rates, patients remaining in hospital, additional bed days lost, delay reasons and discharge destinations.

The analysis showed that discharge-flow pressure increased toward the end of the financial year, with the national NCTR not-discharged rate rising from 55.8% in Q1 to 59.2% in Q4. Regional, ICB and provider-level analysis showed substantial variation across the system.

Additional bed days analysis highlighted the capacity impact of delayed discharge, with more than 6.7 million LOS 7+ additional bed days recorded nationally across 52 weekly reporting points. Delay reason analysis suggested that discharge delays were shaped by a combination of onward-care capacity constraints, hospital-community interface processes and internal hospital processes.

This project completes the third phase of the NHS patient-flow analytics portfolio, complementing earlier analysis of A&E performance and bed occupancy. Together, the three projects provide a wider view of hospital flow, from urgent care demand to inpatient capacity and discharge flow.

Future Work¶

Future work could link discharge delay findings with A&E performance and bed occupancy metrics to explore wider patient-flow pressures across the hospital system.

Further analysis could also examine provider-level trends over time, compare discharge delays with regional bed occupancy, and explore whether high discharge delay areas also experience higher urgent and emergency care pressure.

Additional external datasets, such as social care capacity, community rehabilitation availability or care home capacity, could help provide more context for the delay reason findings.

Final Output Check¶

The final check confirms that the analysis and Tableau-ready files have been created successfully.

In [82]:
print("Final project outputs created successfully.")

print("\nAnalysis tables:")
for file in sorted(os.listdir(tables_path)):
    print("-", file)

print("\nTableau-ready files:")
for file in sorted(os.listdir(tableau_path)):
    print("-", file)
